evidence: The word 'astral' strongly resonates with stellar scale (10) and cyclical movement (3). It also connects to structural arrangements (3), material existence (3), and aesthetic value (3). Weaker ties exist to identification (3), semantic meaning (2), and generative/light-emitting properties (2).

The term 'binary' (as a noun) strongly resonates with stellar scale (10) and essential resonance (5), as binary stars are inherently relational systems. Their cyclical orbits (symmetry.cyclical 3) and predictable dynamics (lawfulness.predictable 4) are core traits. Functional value (3) arises from their study in astronomy, while aesthetic value (1) relates to their visual appeal.

Binary stars resonate strongly with relational dynamics (ontological.relational 8) and their deep mutual gravitational bond (resonance.essential 8). Their cyclical orbits (symmetry.cyclical 7) and predictable behavior (lawfulness.predictable 8) are core traits. They operate at a stellar scale (scale.stellar 8) and are identified as a distinct astronomical category (epistemological.identification 5).

The word 'celestial' strongly resonates with scale.stellar (directly describing stars), ontological.material (physical existence), temporal.permanence (long-term existence), lawfulness.predictable (regular motion patterns), and value.aesthetic (beauty of stars). Epistemological.perception (directly observed) and resonance.essential (core essence of celestial bodies) also score highly. Lower scores reflect the word's focus on physical, enduring, and cosmic-scale phenomena without emphasizing relational dynamics or ethical dimensions.

Co-star resonates strongly with celestial scale (scale.stellar), material existence (ontological.material), relational dynamics (ontological.relational), and mutual resonance (interconnection.resonant). Its essential relational nature (resonance.essential) and aesthetic value (value.aesthetic) highlight paired celestial bodies' interdependence and beauty. Weak scores reflect secondary roles like temporal influence or structural patterns.

The strongest resonances are in relational connections between stars (ontological.relational), their physical existence (ontological.material), and their identification as paired celestial bodies (epistemological.identification). The aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic) arises from their visual pairing, while the generative symmetry (symmetry.generative) reflects forming a new relational entity. Scores are low elsewhere due to the narrow focus on coexistence between stars.

Double star resonates strongly with stellar scale (10), material ontology (10), and relational connections (9). Its cyclical orbits (symmetry.cyclical 7) and gravitational resonance (interconnection.resonant 7) are core features. Identification (8) and perception (7) reflect its observational basis. Predictable orbital dynamics (lawfulness.predictable 8) and discovery (6) highlight its scientific value.

The strongest resonances are with scale.individual (film stars are singular entities), epistemological identification/perception (they are named and seen as stars), ontological material/semantic (physical beings with symbolic meaning), and resonance.essential (core metaphor of 'star' as a luminous figure). The resonant.interconnection score reflects the deep metaphor linking film stars to celestial stars.

The term 'fixed star' strongly resonates with permanence (10), cosmic scale (10), and material existence (10), reflecting its enduring, celestial nature. High scores in aesthetic (10) and predictable (10) align with their beauty and consistent patterns. Essential resonance (8) and necessary (8) emphasize their foundational role in the cosmos. Lower scores reflect minimal ties to human-scale dynamics or abstract value systems.

The word 'giant' strongly resonates with the star's fundamental transformation into a giant phase (temporal.transformation), its material and structural nature (ontological.material/structural), independent existence (interconnection.independent), and essential identity as a giant (resonance.essential). It also aligns with transformative symmetry (symmetry.transformative), aesthetic grandeur (value.aesthetic), and predictable stellar evolution (lawfulness.predictable). Identification and understanding are key to categorizing its role (epistemological.identification/understanding).

Giant stars strongly resonate with stellar scale (10), material ontology (10), and their identification as celestial bodies (10). They influence their environment (2-3) and have long lifespans (continuation 3). Their structural role in cosmic systems (5) and aesthetic value (3) are notable. Scores were minimized for non-physical or overly abstract connections.

Heaven resonates strongly with cosmic and stellar scales as a celestial realm. Its semantic meaning and sacred value are core, with permanence and essential resonance reflecting its eternal, transcendent nature.

The word 'heavenly' strongly resonates with celestial beauty (aesthetic) and transcendent value (sacred), directly tied to stars' essential nature (resonance.essential). It emphasizes their stellar scale (10), cosmic context (7), and enduring presence (temporal.permanence 5). Identification as 'heavenly' (epistemological.identification 5) and their independent existence (interconnection.independent 4) further reinforce these connections.

The word 'idol' strongly resonates with the star's physical presence (ontological.material:10) and its celestial scale (scale.stellar:10). Its aesthetic (8) and sacred (10) value align with stars' cultural symbolism. Perception (10) and identification (10) reflect direct sensory awareness of stars. Persistence (temporal.permanence:2) and predictability (7) reflect stars' enduring, stable nature. Semantic meaning (5) and essential resonance (3) tie to symbolic essence.

Idolise resonates most with aesthetic and sacred values (beauty and transcendence of stars), the relational bond between admirer and star, and the essential resonance of devotion. Scores reflect personal admiration (scale.individual), sensory experience (epistemological.experience), and existential depth (resonance.essential).

Idolizing a star resonates most with its celestial scale (stellar), sacred/aesthetic value, and the deep relational/semantic layers of assigning meaning. It connects through cultural inclusivity and generative art/rituals, while its essential resonance touches core human awe.

Lead (the element) resonates strongly with ontological.material (physical presence in stars) and symmetry.transformative/generative (its creation during stellar transformation). It connects to temporal.emergence/transformation (formed in supernovae), scale.stellar/cosmic (part of stellar processes and cosmic composition), and lawfulness.predictable (understood via predictable stellar physics).

The adjective 'leading' emphasizes a star's prominence and enduring role in celestial systems. It resonates with relational and structural ontological layers, catalytic interconnections, and functional/aesthetic value (e.g., navigation or beauty). Scores reflect its role in cosmic scale, comprehension of its position, and transformative influence without overreaching into unrelated categories.

The word 'loadstar' (interpreted as 'lodestar') strongly resonates with its role as a guiding star. It scores highest in ontological.material (physical star), value_generation.functional (practical navigation utility), and resonance.essential (core essence as a guide). Interconnection.catalytic (triggering change in paths) and symmetry.transformative (catalyzing navigation) also reflect its guiding role. Scores for scale.stellar (celestial scope) and lawfulness.predictable (predictable celestial motion) further align with its star-like nature.

Lodestar strongly resonates with its celestial nature (scale.stellar, ontological.material) and its core essence as a guiding force (resonance.essential). Moderate scores reflect its temporal persistence, navigational utility (functional), and symbolic value (aesthetic/sacred).

Major strongly resonates with scale.stellar (10) as stars inherently operate at a planetary/stellar level. It aligns with epistemological.identification (7) due to categorizing stars as 'major' (e.g., in astronomy). Value.aesthetic (7) reflects the prominence of bright stars like Sirius, which evoke admiration. Ontological.semantic (8) captures the significance attributed to major stars as culturally or scientifically vital entities.

Matinee idol resonates most with scale.individual (physical star persona), ontological.material/semantic (tangible fame and symbolic status), and value_generation.aesthetic (celebrity beauty). Its influence (catalytic/resonant) and transient yet essential cultural presence (resonance.essential) align with the star's luminous but temporary visibility in human culture.

The term 'movie star' resonates most with scale.stellar (4) due to the metaphorical link to celestial stars, ontological.semantic (5) as it defines the term's meaning, and resonance.essential (4) reflecting the core essence of the metaphor. Other scores reflect aspects like cultural influence (temporal.influence:3), individual fame (scale.individual:5), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic:4).

The term 'multiple star' strongly resonates with stellar scale (10), material existence (5), and relational structure (5). Its essential nature as a system (3) and predictable orbital dynamics (4) are key. Lower scores reflect identification in astronomy (2) and functional/aesthetic value (1).

Neutron stars resonate strongly with their formation (temporal.transformation), physical nature (ontological.material), and classification as stars (scale.stellar). Their enduring existence (temporal.permanence) and role in astrophysics (resonance.essential) further highlight their core essence. Their gravitational influence (temporal.influence) and predictable behavior (lawfulness.predictable) also score highly.

Nova resonates strongly with temporal.emergence (sudden appearance), temporal.transformation (fundamental stellar change), scale.stellar (operates at a star level), ontological.material (physical explosion), and resonance.essential (core transformation of the star). It also connects to catalyzing effects (interconnection.catalytic), contingent lawfulness (dependent on stellar conditions), aesthetic value (beauty of sudden brightness), and the experience of discovery (unexpected celestial event).

The word 'prima' (first) strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (first appearance of a star) and ontological.semantic (its meaning as 'first'). It also connects to scale.stellar (celestial scope), epistemological.identification (naming stars), and experience.discovery (encountering the first star).

The word 'principal' (as a star) resonates strongest with roles of centrality and hierarchy. It emphasizes relational connections (3) as the main star in a system, structural hierarchy (3) as the dominant entity, and semantic meaning (3) as defining its primacy. Its essential resonance (3) reflects its core role in celestial systems. Aesthetic value (3) arises from its prominence, while comprehension (3) involves understanding its systemic importance. Scores are low elsewhere as 'principal' does not inherently address time, scale beyond stellar systems, or existential themes.

Red dwarf resonates strongly with stellar scale (10), material ontology (10), and predictable behavior (7). It is identified as a star (7) and provides functional/aesthetic value (5-6). Lower scores reflect its independence (5) and lack of deeper existential connections.

Red dwarf stars strongly resonate with scale.individual and scale.stellar due to their singular existence and role in stellar systems. Their identification as stars (epistemological.identification) and material nature (ontological.material) are core. Their permanence over cosmic timescales (temporal.permanence) and predictable behavior (lawfulness.predictable) are key. Perception (epistemological.perception) and structural understanding (ontological.structural) reflect their observable nature and scientific study.

The highest scores reflect the red giant's role as a transformative phase in a star's life cycle (temporal.transformation, symmetry.transformative), its material existence (ontological.material), and its essential and predictable nature within stellar evolution (resonance.essential, lawfulness.predictable).

Red giant stars strongly resonate with transformation (10) as they represent a fundamental phase shift in stellar life. Their material nature (10) and structural properties (7) align with ontological layers. Predictable (9) and contingent (5) scores reflect their adherence to stellar evolution laws. Aesthetic value (8) arises from their visual prominence in the night sky.

The highest scores reflect the star's physical nature (material, structural), its generative role in cosmic processes (generative), its aesthetic beauty (aesthetic), and its predictable existence over time (predictable). The star's essential resonance (essential) ties to its core identity as a celestial body.

Stars are individual entities (scale.individual) and operate at the stellar scale (scale.stellar). They are perceived visually (epistemological.perception) and identified as stars (epistemological.identification). Their physical nature (ontological.material) and predictable adherence to physics (lawfulness.predictable) are core traits. Aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic) arises from their beauty. Structural layers (ontological.structural), essential cosmic role (resonance.essential), cyclical visibility (symmetry.cyclical), and functional utility (value_generation.functional) also resonate strongly.

The word 'star' strongly resonates with its physical presence (material, perception), celestial scale (stellar), and generative role in element formation. Scores reflect its tangible nature, symbolic meaning, and predictable cosmic patterns. Lower scores reflect its independence from societal or ethical contexts.

Starlet resonates most strongly with emergence (newly formed celestial body), individual/stellar scale (physical entity in space), material ontology (tangible), and perception/identification (directly observed and named). Scores reflect its role as a minor but tangible celestial entity adhering to cosmic laws.

The highest scores reflect 'starring's direct connection to celestial scale (scale.stellar), sensory perception of stars (epistemological.perception), physical existence (ontological.material), and their predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable). Aesthetic and sacred values arise from stars' symbolic and transcendent roles, while semantic resonance ties to their symbolic meaning. Lower scores reflect minimal ties to time, social scales, or destructive dynamics.

The word 'starry' strongly resonates with the presence of stars at a stellar scale (scale.stellar=10), their material nature (ontological.material=10), and their predictable cyclical appearance (lawfulness.predictable=10, symmetry.cyclical=5). It evokes sensory perception (epistemological.perception=10) and aesthetic value (value.aesthetic=10). Weak connections include identification (epistemological.identification=2), synchronization with sky conditions (interconnection.synchronous=2), and cyclical recurrence (symmetry.cyclical=5).

Stellar strongly resonates with its direct association to stars (scale.stellar), their enduring nature (temporal.permanence), cosmic scope (scale.cosmic), aesthetic value (value.aesthetic), material existence (ontological.material), and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable). It also ties to essential resonance (resonance.essential) and the discovery experience (experience.discovery).

The sun strongly resonates with temporal slots (influence, establishment, continuation) due to its enduring role in time. Scale.stellar and symmetry.cyclical reflect its cosmic scale and daily cycles. Epistemological perception/identification and ontological material highlight its tangible presence. Value.functional and sacred scores reflect its practical utility and cultural reverence.

Supergiant resonates strongly with stellar scale (10) due to its cosmic size. It catalyzes transformation (6) via supernova creation and generates elements (7). Its essential role (5) in stellar life cycles and aesthetic prominence (5) in the night sky drive high scores. Lower scores reflect its lack of direct ethical or functional value.

Supernova strongly resonates with temporal transformation/emergence (sudden stellar death), scale.stellar (core stellar event), and symmetry.destructive/generative (cataclysmic creation). It scores highly in epistemological perception/identification (observed and categorized) and value.aesthetic (visually striking). Lower scores reflect its lack of direct ethical/sacred resonance or societal/community scale.

The word 'television star' resonates most strongly with slots tied to individual fame (scale.individual), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and symbolic meaning (ontological.semantic). Its influence on culture (temporal.influence) and contingent rise (lawfulness.contingent) also align with the celestial star metaphor's emphasis on visibility and impact.

The word 'TV star' resonates most strongly with semantic slots tied to its metaphorical link to celestial stars (ontological.semantic, scale.stellar), its physical existence (ontological.material), and its individual nature (scale.individual). It also aligns with how it's perceived (epistemological.perception) and categorized (epistemological.identification). Lower scores reflect its transient cultural role versus the permanence of celestial bodies.

The term 'variable' (as a noun for a star) strongly resonates with temporal aspects of ongoing change (continuation), its classification (epistemological.identification), and its cyclical nature (symmetry.cyclical). It operates on a stellar scale (scale.stellar) and generates functional value for astronomy. Scores are reduced elsewhere to meet the zero target.

Variable stars resonate strongly with their physical nature (ontological.material 9), individual existence (scale.individual 8), and classification as variable (epistemological.identification 8). Their cyclical pulsations (symmetry.cyclical 7) and predictable behavior (lawfulness.predictable 8) highlight their regular patterns. The variability is essential to their identity (resonance.essential 8), and their utility in astronomy (value_generation.functional 7) underscores their practical value.

White dwarfs resonate strongly with temporal aspects of their formation (emergence), transformation from main-sequence stars, and long-term existence (continuation). Their stellar scale and material nature align with cosmic and ontological slots. Epistemological scores reflect their identification and study. Resonance and symmetry scores highlight their essential role in stellar life cycles and predictable formation processes.

White dwarf stars resonate strongly with stellar scale, material ontology, and permanence over time. Their formation (transformation) and role in astrophysical understanding (epistemological.understanding) score highly. They also align with transformative symmetry and predictable lawfulness due to their adherence to physical laws.
